one_line_thesis: Externally managed commercial mortgage REIT with a clean loan book (66% weighted average loan-to-value, zero realized losses, no central-business-district office exposure) trading at 0.58 times book value on sector-wide credit fear and a one-quarter earnings dip from unredeployed rights-offering capital.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Seven Hills Realty Trust lends against middle-market commercial property at a weighted average loan-to-value of 66% and has recorded no realized credit losses. A December 2025 capital raise temporarily diluted distributable earnings to $0.24 per share this quarter, but $100 million of cash and unfunded commitments still need to be invested at roughly 7.8% yields, which should lift that figure back toward the $0.28 run rate over the next year or two. At $7.83 per share, a 46% discount to book value, we expect about 17.65% per year over seven years on a base case that assumes only a partial n...

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Capital cycle and normalized industry economics
failure_modes: replacement cost used for assets that cannot earn their cost of capital
method_fit.rule: Power Zones select the fitting economic method and eligible independent reviewers; they never choose the valuation answer or capital decision.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: Component changes are classified from observable differences in quantity, comparable method, probability, timing, capital, evidence, and schedule scope.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

timeline.label: Q1 2026: net income $4.4M ($0.19 diluted per share); distributable earnings $5.3M ($0.24 per share, 117% payout ratio); book value per share $14.47; cash fell to $56.6M as rights-offering capital deployed

Q4 2025 / FY2025: net income $4.8M ($0.29 per share); distributable earnings $4.6M ($0.28 per share, 100% payout ratio); FY2025 net income $15.4M ($1.00 per share); base management fees $4.36M, incentive fees $0.63M
UBS Master Repurchase Facility maturity extended to February 2028
Wells Fargo Master Repurchase Facility maturity extended to March 2028; maximum facility size increased by $125M to $250M
Rights offering closed: $65.2M gross proceeds, 7.5M new common shares issued, raising shares outstanding roughly 50%
Predecessor RMR Mortgage Trust deregistered as an investment company and converted to a commercial mortgage REIT

industry.structure: SEVN is a small-cap commercial mortgage REIT that originates first mortgage loans on middle-market transitional commercial real estate, externally managed by Tremont Realty Capital, a subsidiary of The RMR Group. The sector includes larger peers financing similar floating-rate bridge loans against office, hotel, multifamily, industrial, and other property types.
industry.share_shift: Not a market-share business in the traditional sense; SEVN's book grows or shrinks based on capital raised and loan originations rather than competitive share gains from peers.
industry.trend: Commercial mortgage REITs broadly trade at discounts to book value amid continued concern about office and other transitional commercial real estate credit, even where individual books such as SEVN's show no realized losses.

top_claims.claim: Seven Hills Realty Trust lends against middle-market commercial property at a weighted average loan-to-value of 66% and has recorded no realized credit losses. A December 2025 capital raise temporarily diluted distributable earnings to $0.24 per share this quarter, but $100 million of cash and unfunded commitments stil

At $8.40 per share, we expect about **14.6%** per year over 7 years on the base case, built on starting distributable earnings of $0.96 per share that we expect to recover toward the pre-raise $1.12 per share annual run rate as new capital is invested.

*Primary risk: A commercial real estate credit shock, particularly in office, causes realized losses, forces a dividend cut, and keeps the price-to-book discount wide or wider for years rather than months.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: A commercial real estate credit shock, particularly in office, causes realized losses, forces a dividend cut, and keeps the price-to-book discount wide or wider for years rather than months.

Office is 23% of the loan portfolio by property type per `SEVN-Q1-2026-Earnings-Presentation.pdf`; even without central-business-district exposure, suburban office credit can deteriorate quickly in a downturn (two office loans already carry a risk rating of 4)

External management fees continue to grow with equity raised even if per-share returns do not, per the fee schedule in `10-K_20260218_rpt20251231_acc0001452477_26_000010.htm`, which weakens the case that management is fully aligned with shareholders

valuation_route.label: Capital cycle and normalized industry economics
